questions.promptHtml: Your startup is launching a mobile app expecting unpredictable traffic spikes during marketing campaigns. The solution must automatically scale compute resources and charge only for actual usage. Which cloud pricing model best fits this requirement?

options.label: Reserved Instances with 3-year commitment
Consumption-based pricing with auto-scaling
Dedicated host pricing with fixed monthly costs

questions.correctLabel: Consumption-based pricing with auto-scaling
questions.extraHtml:
Consumption-based pricing charges only for resources actually consumed, ideal for unpredictable workloads. Reserved Instances require predictable, steady workloads for cost savings. Dedicated hosts charge fixed costs regardless of usage, eliminating cost flexibility.

Reference: Azure consumption-based pricing

questions.promptHtml: A financial services company must keep sensitive customer data within their on-premises datacenter for regulatory compliance, but wants to use Azure services for application hosting and analytics. Which cloud model satisfies these requirements?

options.label: Public cloud with Azure regions selected by geography
Hybrid cloud with on-premises data storage
Private cloud with isolated Azure subscriptions

questions.correctLabel: Hybrid cloud with on-premises data storage
questions.extraHtml:
Hybrid cloud combines on-premises infrastructure (for regulated data) with public cloud services (for compute/analytics). Public cloud alone doesn't provide on-premises control. Private cloud in Azure still stores data in Microsoft datacenters, not on-premises.

Reference: Azure hybrid cloud architecture

questions.promptHtml: Your organization runs a legacy application requiring direct OS-level access to install custom kernel modules and specific network drivers. Which cloud service type provides the necessary control?

options.label: Software as a Service (SaaS)
Platform as a Service (PaaS)
Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS)

questions.correctLabel: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS)
questions.extraHtml:
IaaS provides full OS-level control, allowing kernel module installation and custom network driver configuration. PaaS abstracts the OS layer, limiting customization. SaaS provides only application access with no infrastructure control.

Reference: Azure service models comparison

questions.promptHtml: A retail company experiences 300% traffic increase during Black Friday sales. Their application must automatically provision additional servers during peak times and deallocate them afterward. Which cloud benefit addresses this requirement?

options.label: High availability through redundant datacenters
Scalability with elastic resource allocation
Security through network isolation

questions.correctLabel: Scalability with elastic resource allocation
questions.extraHtml:
Scalability enables automatic resource expansion during peak demand and contraction during low usage, optimizing costs. High availability ensures uptime but doesn't address dynamic capacity needs. Security through isolation doesn't relate to traffic-based scaling.

Reference: Azure scalability patterns

questions.promptHtml: Under the shared responsibility model, your company deploys a web application using Azure App Service (PaaS). Who is responsible for patching the underlying operating system?

options.label: Customer is fully responsible
Microsoft is fully responsible
Shared responsibility between customer and Microsoft

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft is fully responsible
questions.extraHtml:
In PaaS models like App Service, Microsoft manages OS patching, infrastructure, and platform updates. Customers manage application code, data, and access controls. In IaaS, OS patching becomes customer responsibility.

Reference: Azure shared responsibility model

questions.promptHtml: A healthcare provider must ensure patient data remains within specific geographic boundaries due to GDPR requirements. Which Azure architectural component addresses data residency requirements?

options.label: Availability zones within a single region
Specific Azure regions with data residency guarantees
Azure Virtual Network subnet configuration

questions.correctLabel: Specific Azure regions with data residency guarantees
questions.extraHtml:
Azure regions provide data residency guarantees, ensuring data stays within geographic boundaries for compliance. Availability zones span within a single region but don't control geographic data placement. VNet subnets control network segmentation, not data residency.

Reference: Azure compliance and data residency

questions.promptHtml: Your organization wants to eliminate upfront infrastructure costs and shift from CapEx to OpEx. Which cloud characteristic enables this financial model change?

options.label: Consumption-based pricing model
High availability with SLA guarantees
Geographic distribution across regions

questions.correctLabel: Consumption-based pricing model
questions.extraHtml:
Consumption-based pricing eliminates upfront capital expenditure, converting infrastructure costs to operational expenses paid as resources are used. High availability relates to uptime, not financial models. Geographic distribution addresses resilience, not cost structure.

Reference: Azure cost management fundamentals

questions.promptHtml: A government agency requires complete control over hardware, virtualization layer, and network infrastructure while using cloud computing. Which cloud model meets these sovereignty requirements?

options.label: Public cloud with encryption enabled
Private cloud with dedicated infrastructure
Community cloud shared with other agencies

questions.correctLabel: Private cloud with dedicated infrastructure
questions.extraHtml:
Private cloud provides dedicated infrastructure with complete organizational control over hardware, virtualization, and networking. Public cloud shares infrastructure with other tenants. Community cloud is shared among specific organizations, reducing individual control.

Reference: Cloud deployment models

questions.promptHtml: An e-commerce platform needs to automatically adjust compute capacity based on real-time shopping cart activity, with costs tied directly to actual resource usage. Which cloud pricing characteristic provides this flexibility?

options.label: Reserved capacity with prepayment discounts
Pay-as-you-go consumption-based billing
Spot instance pricing with variable availability

questions.correctLabel: Pay-as-you-go consumption-based billing
questions.extraHtml:
Pay-as-you-go consumption billing charges only for resources actively used, aligning costs with actual demand. Reserved capacity requires upfront commitment. Spot instances offer discounts but can be interrupted, unsuitable for production e-commerce.

Reference: Azure pricing models

questions.promptHtml: Your application requires zero downtime during Azure datacenter maintenance. Which architectural component provides infrastructure-level fault isolation within a region?

options.label: Availability zones with zone-redundant deployment
Region pairs for disaster recovery
Resource groups for logical organization

questions.correctLabel: Availability zones with zone-redundant deployment
questions.extraHtml:
Availability zones provide physically separate datacenters within a region with independent power, cooling, and networking, enabling zero downtime during maintenance. Region pairs are for disaster recovery across regions. Resource groups are logical containers without fault isolation.

Reference: Azure availability zones

questions.promptHtml: A financial application demands automatic failover to a secondary region if the primary region becomes unavailable. Which Azure architectural feature provides georedundancy for regional failures?

options.label: Availability sets within a datacenter
Azure region pairs with replication
Management groups for organizing subscriptions

questions.correctLabel: Azure region pairs with replication
questions.extraHtml:
Region pairs provide regional redundancy with data replication to a secondary region for disaster recovery. Availability sets protect against datacenter-level failures within a region. Management groups organize subscriptions but don't provide redundancy.

Reference: Azure region pairs

questions.promptHtml: Your organization has multiple departments requiring isolated billing, separate access controls, and independent resource quotas. Which Azure organizational component provides this logical boundary?

options.label: Resource groups within a single subscription
Separate Azure subscriptions per department
Virtual networks with network security groups

questions.correctLabel: Separate Azure subscriptions per department
questions.extraHtml:
Subscriptions provide billing boundaries, independent access controls, and separate resource quotas. Resource groups organize resources within a subscription but share billing and quotas. VNets provide network isolation, not billing or access control boundaries.

Reference: Azure subscription organization

questions.promptHtml: A global enterprise with 50 business units needs centralized policy enforcement for security standards while delegating cost management to each unit. Which Azure component enables this hierarchical governance?

options.label: Management groups with inherited policies
Network security groups with centralized rules
Resource locks preventing modifications

questions.correctLabel: Management groups with inherited policies
questions.extraHtml:
Management groups provide hierarchical organization of subscriptions with policy inheritance, enabling centralized governance while allowing delegation. NSGs control network traffic, not organizational governance. Resource locks prevent changes but don't provide hierarchical policy inheritance.

Reference: Azure management groups

questions.promptHtml: Your web application needs to process customer requests with guaranteed response times during traffic spikes, automatically scaling from 2 to 50 instances. Which Azure compute service provides automatic scaling without VM management?

options.label: Azure Virtual Machines with manual scaling
Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets with autoscale rules
Azure Container Instances with fixed replicas

questions.correctLabel: Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets with autoscale rules
questions.extraHtml:
Virtual Machine Scale Sets automatically scale VMs based on metrics while abstracting VM management overhead. Standard VMs require manual scaling. Container Instances don't provide built-in autoscaling with metric-based rules like VMSS.

Reference: Azure VMSS overview

questions.promptHtml: A development team needs to run event-driven code that executes only when storage blobs are uploaded, with zero cost during idle periods. Which compute service fits this serverless requirement?

options.label: Azure Virtual Machines with scheduled start/stop
Azure Functions with consumption plan
Azure Kubernetes Service with node autoscaling

questions.correctLabel: Azure Functions with consumption plan
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Functions consumption plan charges only during execution with automatic scaling and zero cost when idle. VMs incur costs even when stopped (storage charges). AKS requires running nodes with associated costs even during low activity.

Reference: Azure Functions consumption plan

questions.promptHtml: Your organization needs to provide 500 remote employees with Windows 11 desktops accessible from any device, with centralized management and security controls. Which Azure service delivers this virtualized desktop experience?

options.label: Azure Virtual Machines with RDP access per user
Azure Virtual Desktop with multi-session Windows 11
Azure App Service for hosting desktop applications

questions.correctLabel: Azure Virtual Desktop with multi-session Windows 11
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Virtual Desktop provides multi-session Windows 11 desktops with centralized management, optimized for remote access. Individual VMs per user are cost-prohibitive. App Service hosts web applications, not full Windows desktops.

Reference: Azure Virtual Desktop

questions.promptHtml: Two Azure virtual networks in different regions need private connectivity without traversing the public internet, with minimal latency. Which networking feature enables this private network connection?

options.label: Virtual network peering across regions
Public IP addresses with VPN client
ExpressRoute to on-premises then back to Azure

questions.correctLabel: Virtual network peering across regions
questions.extraHtml:
VNet peering provides private connectivity between virtual networks using Azure backbone network with low latency. Public IPs route traffic over internet. ExpressRoute connects on-premises to Azure, not efficient for VNet-to-VNet communication.

Reference: Azure VNet peering

questions.promptHtml: Your on-premises datacenter requires a dedicated, private connection to Azure with predictable network performance and 10 Gbps bandwidth. Which Azure networking service provides this private connectivity?

options.label: Site-to-site VPN over public internet
ExpressRoute with private peering
Point-to-site VPN for individual connections

questions.correctLabel: ExpressRoute with private peering
questions.extraHtml:
ExpressRoute provides dedicated private connectivity with predictable performance and high bandwidth. Site-to-site VPN uses public internet with variable performance. Point-to-site VPN is for individual user connections, not datacenter connectivity.

Reference: Azure ExpressRoute

questions.promptHtml: A web application backend in Azure needs to be accessible only from the front-end tier within the virtual network, never from the public internet. Which endpoint type ensures this private-only access?

options.label: Public endpoint with firewall rules
Private endpoint within the VNet
Service endpoint with VNet service tags

questions.correctLabel: Private endpoint within the VNet
questions.extraHtml:
Private endpoints expose services exclusively within a VNet using private IP addresses, eliminating public internet exposure. Public endpoints with firewall rules still expose services to internet. Service endpoints secure traffic but services remain on public IPs.

Reference: Azure private endpoints

questions.promptHtml: Your company stores 200 TB of regulatory compliance documents that must be retained for 7 years but accessed less than once annually. Which Azure Storage tier minimizes costs for this archival scenario?

options.label: Hot tier with standard performance
Cool tier with infrequent access pricing
Archive tier with long-term retention pricing

questions.correctLabel: Archive tier with long-term retention pricing
questions.extraHtml:
Archive tier offers lowest storage costs for data accessed less than twice per year with acceptable retrieval latency. Hot tier is for frequently accessed data. Cool tier is for infrequent access (monthly), not annual archival needs.

Reference: Azure Storage access tiers

questions.promptHtml: A mission-critical application requires storage that can automatically failover to a secondary region with zero data loss if the primary region fails. Which Azure Storage redundancy option provides this synchronous geo-replication?

options.label: Locally redundant storage (LRS) with three copies
Geo-redundant storage (GRS) with async replication
Geo-zone-redundant storage (GZRS) with sync and async replication

questions.correctLabel: Geo-zone-redundant storage (GZRS) with sync and async replication
questions.extraHtml:
GZRS combines zone-redundancy (synchronous within region) with geo-redundancy (asynchronous to secondary region), providing highest durability. LRS only protects within a datacenter. GRS provides geo-redundancy but without zone-level protection in primary region.

Reference: Azure Storage redundancy options

questions.promptHtml: You need to migrate 80 TB of archival data from on-premises to Azure Blob Storage, but internet bandwidth is limited to 10 Mbps. Which Azure service enables physical data transfer via shipped storage appliance?

options.label: AzCopy over internet connection
Azure Data Box for offline data transfer
Azure File Sync for continuous replication

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Box for offline data transfer
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Data Box ships physical storage devices for offline data transfer when network bandwidth is insufficient. AzCopy requires network bandwidth. Azure File Sync is for ongoing file synchronization, not initial bulk migration of 80 TB.

Reference: Azure Data Box

questions.promptHtml: Your application authenticates users from multiple external identity providers including Google, Facebook, and on-premises Active Directory. Which Azure identity service federates these external identities?

options.label: Microsoft Entra ID with external identities
Microsoft Entra Domain Services with forest trusts
Azure Active Directory B2C for consumer identities

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Entra ID with external identities
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) supports external identities through federation with social and enterprise identity providers. Entra Domain Services provides domain join capabilities, not federation. B2C is consumer-focused but Entra ID handles broader external identity scenarios.

Reference: Azure external identities

questions.promptHtml: Users must authenticate with both password and smartphone verification before accessing sensitive financial data. Which Azure security feature enforces this second authentication factor?

options.label: Single sign-on (SSO) for seamless authentication
Multifactor authentication (MFA) requiring two factors
Conditional Access policies based on device compliance

questions.correctLabel: Multifactor authentication (MFA) requiring two factors
questions.extraHtml:
MFA requires two or more verification methods (password + phone), strengthening authentication security. SSO provides convenience but doesn't enforce multiple factors. Conditional Access enforces access policies but doesn't inherently require multiple authentication factors.

Reference: Azure MFA

questions.promptHtml: Access to production databases should be granted based on job role rather than individual user assignments, simplifying permission management. Which Azure access control mechanism implements this role-based approach?

options.label: Shared access signatures (SAS) with delegation
Azure role-based access control (RBAC) with role assignments
Network security groups (NSGs) restricting IP addresses

questions.correctLabel: Azure role-based access control (RBAC) with role assignments
questions.extraHtml:
Azure RBAC assigns permissions through roles (Contributor, Reader, Owner) rather than individual grants, simplifying management. SAS tokens provide resource-specific access, not role-based. NSGs control network traffic, not identity-based access.

Reference: Azure RBAC

questions.promptHtml: Your security policy requires verifying every access request based on user identity, device health, and location before granting access to resources. Which security approach implements this continuous verification?

options.label: Perimeter-based security with firewall rules
Zero Trust architecture with conditional verification
Defense-in-depth with layered security controls

questions.correctLabel: Zero Trust architecture with conditional verification
questions.extraHtml:
Zero Trust assumes breach and verifies every access request using identity, device, location, and other signals. Perimeter-based security trusts internal network access. Defense-in-depth provides layers but doesn't inherently enforce continuous verification.

Reference: Zero Trust security

questions.promptHtml: You need unified visibility across Azure resources, AWS workloads, and on-premises servers for security recommendations and threat detection. Which Azure service provides this multi-cloud security management?

options.label: Azure Firewall for network traffic inspection
Microsoft Defender for Cloud for cloud security posture management
Azure Security Center classic for Azure-only monitoring

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Defender for Cloud for cloud security posture management
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft Defender for Cloud provides unified security management across Azure, AWS, GCP, and on-premises with security recommendations and threat protection. Azure Firewall is network-specific. Security Center classic was Azure-only and is now Defender for Cloud.

Reference: Microsoft Defender for Cloud

questions.promptHtml: Your organization enforces a security strategy with multiple protective layers including network firewalls, application gateways, identity controls, and encryption. Which security principle describes this layered approach?

options.label: Defense-in-depth with multiple security layers
Zero Trust with continuous verification
Least privilege with minimal access rights

questions.correctLabel: Defense-in-depth with multiple security layers
questions.extraHtml:
Defense-in-depth implements multiple security layers ensuring if one layer fails, others provide protection. Zero Trust focuses on continuous verification. Least privilege grants minimum necessary access but isn't inherently layered.

Reference: Defense-in-depth security

questions.promptHtml: An application requires conditional access that blocks sign-ins from unmanaged devices while allowing access from corporate-managed devices. Which Entra feature enforces these device-based access policies?

options.label: Microsoft Entra Domain Services with group policies
Microsoft Entra Conditional Access with device compliance
Azure RBAC with custom role definitions

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access with device compliance
questions.extraHtml:
Entra Conditional Access enforces policies based on conditions like device compliance, location, and risk level. Domain Services provides directory services but not conditional access policies. RBAC controls resource permissions, not conditional authentication.

Reference: Entra Conditional Access

questions.promptHtml: A legacy application requires traditional domain join and group policy management in Azure. Which Azure identity service provides these Active Directory Domain Services capabilities?

options.label: Microsoft Entra ID with cloud-only authentication
Microsoft Entra Domain Services with managed domain controllers
Azure RBAC with built-in roles

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Entra Domain Services with managed domain controllers
questions.extraHtml:
Entra Domain Services provides managed domain controllers supporting domain join, LDAP, and group policies for legacy applications. Entra ID is cloud-native without traditional AD capabilities. Azure RBAC controls resource access, not domain services.

Reference: Entra Domain Services

questions.promptHtml: Users should sign in once with corporate credentials and automatically access Office 365, Salesforce, and internal applications without re-entering passwords. Which authentication feature enables this seamless experience?

options.label: Multifactor authentication (MFA) for enhanced security
Single sign-on (SSO) for unified authentication
Passwordless authentication with biometrics

questions.correctLabel: Single sign-on (SSO) for unified authentication
questions.extraHtml:
SSO allows users to authenticate once and access multiple applications without re-authentication. MFA enhances security but requires multiple factors. Passwordless eliminates passwords but doesn't inherently provide cross-application SSO.

Reference: Azure SSO

questions.promptHtml: Your application experiences variable CPU usage ranging from 20% to 95% throughout the day. Management wants detailed cost recommendations to optimize spending. Which Azure tool provides cost optimization suggestions?

options.label: Azure Monitor for performance metrics
Azure Advisor for cost recommendations
Azure Cost Management for spending analysis

questions.correctLabel: Azure Advisor for cost recommendations
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Advisor provides personalized best practice recommendations including cost optimization suggestions like rightsizing VMs. Azure Monitor tracks metrics but doesn't provide recommendations. Cost Management analyzes spending but Advisor provides actionable recommendations.

Reference: Azure Advisor cost recommendations

questions.promptHtml: Before deploying new infrastructure, you need to estimate monthly costs for 10 VMs, 5 TB storage, and bandwidth usage. Which Azure tool provides cost forecasting before resource deployment?

options.label: Azure Cost Management for analyzing existing spending
Azure Pricing Calculator for estimating future costs
Azure Advisor for optimization recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Pricing Calculator for estimating future costs
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Pricing Calculator estimates costs for planned resources before deployment. Cost Management analyzes existing resource spending. Azure Advisor recommends optimizations for deployed resources, not cost estimation for planned deployments.

Reference: Azure Pricing Calculator

questions.promptHtml: Your finance team needs to allocate Azure spending across departments for chargeback reporting. Which Azure feature enables cost categorization and allocation tracking?

options.label: Resource groups for organizing related resources
Tags with department metadata for cost allocation
Management groups for subscription hierarchy

questions.correctLabel: Tags with department metadata for cost allocation
questions.extraHtml:
Tags provide metadata for resources enabling cost allocation, chargeback, and filtering in Cost Management. Resource groups organize resources but don't provide cost allocation metadata. Management groups organize subscriptions but tags enable granular cost tracking.

Reference: Azure cost allocation with tags

questions.promptHtml: Your organization must prevent accidental deletion of production resources while allowing authorized updates to configurations. Which Azure feature prevents unintended deletions while permitting modifications?

options.label: Azure RBAC removing delete permissions
Resource locks with CanNotDelete setting
Azure Policy blocking delete operations

questions.correctLabel: Resource locks with CanNotDelete setting
questions.extraHtml:
Resource locks with CanNotDelete prevent deletion while allowing updates. RBAC removing delete permissions prevents deletions but affects authorized users. Azure Policy can audit or deny but resource locks provide explicit deletion protection.

Reference: Azure resource locks

questions.promptHtml: Your compliance team requires that all Azure storage accounts must use encryption and be tagged with a data classification label. Which Azure service enforces these organizational standards?

options.label: Azure Policy with built-in and custom policy definitions
Azure RBAC with read-only roles
Resource locks preventing changes

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with built-in and custom policy definitions
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Policy enforces organizational standards through policy definitions that audit or deny non-compliant resources. RBAC controls access, not compliance standards. Resource locks prevent changes but don't enforce compliance requirements.

Reference: Azure Policy

questions.promptHtml: Your organization needs unified data governance across Azure, on-premises SQL databases, and third-party SaaS applications for data classification and lineage. Which Azure service provides this multi-source data governance?

options.label: Azure Policy for resource compliance
Microsoft Purview for data governance and cataloging
Azure Monitor for data observability

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Purview for data governance and cataloging
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft Purview provides unified data governance across multi-cloud and on-premises environments with data discovery, classification, and lineage. Azure Policy governs resource configurations, not data. Azure Monitor tracks metrics, not data governance.

Reference: Microsoft Purview

questions.promptHtml: Your infrastructure requires consistent deployment of standardized virtual networks across multiple subscriptions using declarative templates. Which Azure tool enables infrastructure-as-code deployments?

options.label: Azure Portal with manual resource creation
Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates with JSON definitions
Azure Cloud Shell for running CLI commands

questions.correctLabel: Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates with JSON definitions
questions.extraHtml:
ARM templates define infrastructure as code using JSON, enabling consistent, repeatable deployments. Azure Portal is manual, not infrastructure-as-code. Cloud Shell executes commands but isn't declarative infrastructure-as-code.

Reference: ARM templates

questions.promptHtml: You need to manage Azure resources using command-line tools from a web browser without installing software locally. Which Azure service provides this browser-based shell experience?

options.label: Azure Portal GUI for point-and-click management
Azure Cloud Shell with embedded Bash and PowerShell
Azure DevOps pipelines for automated deployments

questions.correctLabel: Azure Cloud Shell with embedded Bash and PowerShell
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Cloud Shell provides browser-based command-line access with pre-installed Azure CLI and PowerShell without local installation. Azure Portal is GUI-based. DevOps pipelines automate deployments but aren't interactive shells.

Reference: Azure Cloud Shell

questions.promptHtml: Your organization manages servers across Azure, AWS, on-premises VMware, and physical datacenters. Management wants unified Azure management capabilities across all environments. Which Azure service extends Azure control plane to external resources?

options.label: Azure Monitor for multi-cloud monitoring
Azure Arc for unified resource management
Azure Migrate for workload migration

questions.correctLabel: Azure Arc for unified resource management
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Arc extends Azure management and services to any infrastructure including on-premises, multi-cloud, and edge. Azure Monitor tracks metrics but doesn't extend management capabilities. Azure Migrate focuses on migration, not ongoing management.

Reference: Azure Arc

questions.promptHtml: Developers manually deploy Azure resources causing configuration drift and inconsistent environments. Management requires standardized, repeatable deployments. Which approach addresses this requirement?

options.label: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with declarative templates
Manual deployments with detailed documentation
Azure Portal with saved resource configurations

questions.correctLabel: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with declarative templates
questions.extraHtml:
Infrastructure as Code uses declarative templates (ARM, Bicep, Terraform) ensuring consistent, version-controlled, repeatable deployments. Manual deployments with documentation still allow drift. Portal saved configurations aren't true IaC.

Reference: Infrastructure as Code

questions.promptHtml: Your application experiences intermittent performance issues. You need to collect, analyze, and visualize application telemetry including request rates, failure rates, and dependency tracking. Which Azure service provides this application performance monitoring?

options.label: Azure Service Health for platform status
Application Insights for application performance management
Azure Advisor for optimization recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Application Insights for application performance management
questions.extraHtml:
Application Insights provides detailed application telemetry with request tracking, dependency mapping, and failure analysis. Service Health reports Azure platform status. Advisor provides recommendations but not real-time telemetry collection.

Reference: Application Insights

questions.promptHtml: You need real-time alerts when virtual machine CPU exceeds 90% for 5 minutes, automatically triggering an email to operations. Which Azure service enables metric-based alerting with actions?

options.label: Azure Monitor alerts with action groups
Azure Service Health for incident notifications
Azure Advisor for performance recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Monitor alerts with action groups
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Monitor alerts trigger on metrics, logs, or activity with action groups executing notifications or automated responses. Service Health reports Azure platform issues, not resource metrics. Advisor provides recommendations but not real-time alerts.

Reference: Azure Monitor alerts

questions.promptHtml: Your management needs visibility into planned Azure maintenance, service incidents, and health advisories across all subscriptions. Which Azure service provides this platform health information?

options.label: Azure Monitor for resource metrics
Azure Service Health for platform status and incidents
Azure Advisor for best practice recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Service Health for platform status and incidents
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Service Health provides personalized service health information including planned maintenance, incidents, and advisories. Azure Monitor tracks resource metrics, not platform health. Advisor provides recommendations, not incident reporting.

Reference: Azure Service Health

questions.promptHtml: You need to query and analyze logs from 1000 VMs to identify security anomalies using custom queries. Which Azure service provides this log aggregation and querying capability?

options.label: Azure Monitor with Log Analytics workspace
Application Insights for application logs only
Azure Advisor for security recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Monitor with Log Analytics workspace
questions.extraHtml:
Log Analytics workspace collects and queries logs from multiple sources using Kusto Query Language (KQL). Application Insights focuses on application telemetry. Advisor provides recommendations but doesn't aggregate or query logs.

Reference: Azure Log Analytics

questions.promptHtml: A startup deploys all resources in a single resource group. As the application grows, they need to organize resources by lifecycle, allowing separate deployment and deletion of frontend and backend components. What architectural change addresses this need?

options.label: Create multiple resource groups by component lifecycle
Use tags to logically separate resources
Deploy components in different Azure regions

questions.correctLabel: Create multiple resource groups by component lifecycle
questions.extraHtml:
Resource groups define lifecycle boundaries enabling independent deployment, management, and deletion. Tags provide metadata but don't affect lifecycle management. Different regions address availability, not lifecycle organization.

Reference: Azure resource groups

questions.promptHtml: An enterprise with 500 subscriptions needs to enforce a policy requiring all resources to have an owner tag. The policy must apply automatically to new subscriptions. Which Azure feature provides this inherited policy governance?

options.label: Azure Policy assigned at management group level
Individual policy assignments per subscription
Resource group tags with inheritance

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy assigned at management group level
questions.extraHtml:
Management groups enable policy assignment that automatically inherits to all child subscriptions and resources. Individual subscription assignments require manual configuration per subscription. Resource tags don't enforce policies.

Reference: Management group policy inheritance

questions.promptHtml: A globally distributed application serves users in North America, Europe, and Asia. Each region requires independent deployments with data residency compliance. Which Azure architectural pattern addresses this multi-region requirement?

options.label: Single region with high availability zones
Multiple regions with separate deployments per geography
Region pairs with automatic failover

questions.correctLabel: Multiple regions with separate deployments per geography
questions.extraHtml:
Multiple regions with independent deployments enable geographic data residency compliance and regional autonomy. Single region violates data residency. Region pairs provide DR but don't ensure data residency per geographic requirement.

Reference: Multi-region architecture

questions.promptHtml: Your application uses Azure VMs, App Services, databases, and storage. You need logical grouping that enables applying access controls, policies, and lifecycle management to all resources collectively. Which Azure construct provides this organizational capability?

options.label: Virtual networks for network grouping
Resource groups for logical resource organization
Availability sets for VM grouping

questions.correctLabel: Resource groups for logical resource organization
questions.extraHtml:
Resource groups logically group resources for collective management including RBAC, policies, and lifecycle. VNets provide network isolation only. Availability sets group VMs for high availability but don't provide broader resource organization.

Reference: Azure resource group benefits

questions.promptHtml: An application requires running microservices with isolated processes, fast startup times, and portability across environments without full VM overhead. Which Azure compute type balances isolation with lightweight operation?

options.label: Azure Virtual Machines with full OS stack
Azure Container Instances for containerized microservices
Azure Functions for serverless code execution

questions.correctLabel: Azure Container Instances for containerized microservices
questions.extraHtml:
Containers provide process isolation with fast startup and portability without full VM overhead. VMs include full OS stack with longer startup times. Functions are event-driven and not designed for long-running microservices.

Reference: Azure Container Instances

questions.promptHtml: Your application workload runs continuously 24/7 with predictable resource usage. Budget requires minimizing compute costs over 3 years. Which pricing model provides maximum cost savings for this stable workload?

options.label: Pay-as-you-go for flexible billing
Reserved Instances with 3-year commitment
Spot Instances with best-effort availability

questions.correctLabel: Reserved Instances with 3-year commitment
questions.extraHtml:
Reserved Instances offer up to 72% discount for 1-3 year commitments on predictable workloads. Pay-as-you-go provides flexibility but higher costs. Spot Instances offer discounts but can be evicted, unsuitable for continuous workloads.

Reference: Azure Reserved Instances

questions.promptHtml: A batch processing workload runs nightly for 4 hours processing analytics. The workload can tolerate interruptions and resume processing. Which compute pricing model minimizes costs for this fault-tolerant batch job?

options.label: Reserved Instances for guaranteed capacity
Spot VMs with up to 90% discount
Pay-as-you-go for flexible usage

questions.correctLabel: Spot VMs with up to 90% discount
questions.extraHtml:
Spot VMs offer up to 90% discount for interruptible, fault-tolerant workloads like batch processing. Reserved Instances require long-term commitment. Pay-as-you-go doesn't provide the cost savings spot instances offer for interruptible workloads.

Reference: Azure Spot VMs

questions.promptHtml: Your web application must maintain 99.95% uptime SLA. A single VM provides 99.9% SLA. Which Azure compute architecture achieves the higher availability requirement?

options.label: Single VM with premium SSD storage
Availability sets with VMs across fault and update domains
Single VM with automated backups

questions.correctLabel: Availability sets with VMs across fault and update domains
questions.extraHtml:
Availability sets distribute VMs across fault domains (power/network) and update domains (maintenance), achieving higher composite SLA. Single VMs regardless of storage or backups provide lower SLA than distributed architectures.

Reference: Azure availability sets

questions.promptHtml: A web application receives 10,000 requests per second, requiring automatic HTTPS termination, load distribution, and web application firewall protection. Which Azure service provides these Layer 7 capabilities?

options.label: Azure Load Balancer for Layer 4 load balancing
Azure Application Gateway with WAF and SSL offload
Azure Traffic Manager for DNS-based routing

questions.correctLabel: Azure Application Gateway with WAF and SSL offload
questions.extraHtml:
Application Gateway provides Layer 7 load balancing with SSL termination, WAF, and URL-based routing. Load Balancer operates at Layer 4 without SSL/WAF. Traffic Manager provides global DNS routing without Layer 7 features.

Reference: Azure Application Gateway

questions.promptHtml: Your application needs to resolve custom domain names like database.internal to private IP addresses within your Azure virtual network. Which Azure service provides this internal DNS resolution?

options.label: Azure public DNS for internet domain hosting
Azure Private DNS zones for VNet name resolution
Azure Traffic Manager for global routing

questions.correctLabel: Azure Private DNS zones for VNet name resolution
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Private DNS provides name resolution within VNets using private IP addresses. Public DNS resolves internet domains. Traffic Manager provides global traffic routing, not internal DNS resolution.

Reference: Azure Private DNS

questions.promptHtml: Two separate Azure subscriptions each have virtual networks that need private connectivity to share resources. The VNets have non-overlapping address spaces. Which networking feature enables this private cross-subscription connectivity?

options.label: VPN Gateway for encrypted connections
Virtual network peering across subscriptions
ExpressRoute for dedicated connectivity

questions.correctLabel: Virtual network peering across subscriptions
questions.extraHtml:
VNet peering connects VNets across subscriptions using Azure backbone with private connectivity. VPN Gateway adds unnecessary encryption overhead for Azure-to-Azure. ExpressRoute connects on-premises, not VNet-to-VNet across subscriptions.

Reference: Cross-subscription VNet peering

questions.promptHtml: Your organization stores backups as block blobs needing high durability but infrequent access. Minimizing storage costs is critical. Which combination of storage tier and redundancy balances cost with durability?

options.label: Hot tier with LRS for low cost
Cool tier with GRS for infrequent access with geo-redundancy
Archive tier with ZRS for maximum durability

questions.correctLabel: Cool tier with GRS for infrequent access with geo-redundancy
questions.extraHtml:
Cool tier with GRS provides cost-effective storage for infrequent access with geographic redundancy for backups. Hot tier is expensive for infrequent access. Archive tier has high retrieval costs and latency unsuitable for backups needing occasional access.

Reference: Storage tier selection guidance

questions.promptHtml: A financial application requires 99.99% uptime with zero data loss if an Azure datacenter fails. Which storage redundancy option provides datacenter-level redundancy within a region?

options.label: Locally redundant storage (LRS) within single datacenter
Zone-redundant storage (ZRS) across availability zones
Geo-redundant storage (GRS) across regions

questions.correctLabel: Zone-redundant storage (ZRS) across availability zones
questions.extraHtml:
ZRS replicates synchronously across three availability zones within a region, protecting against datacenter failures with zero data loss. LRS only protects within a datacenter. GRS protects across regions but uses asynchronous replication.

Reference: Azure ZRS

questions.promptHtml: Your application needs shared file storage accessible from multiple Windows servers via SMB protocol. Which Azure Storage service provides this shared file system?

options.label: Azure Blob Storage for object storage
Azure Files with SMB protocol support
Azure Queue Storage for message queuing

questions.correctLabel: Azure Files with SMB protocol support
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Files provides fully managed shared file storage accessible via SMB protocol. Blob Storage is object storage without file system protocols. Queue Storage is for message queuing, not shared file access.

Reference: Azure Files

questions.promptHtml: You need to synchronize 10 TB of frequently accessed files from on-premises file servers to Azure Files, maintaining on-premises cache for performance. Which Azure service enables this hybrid file synchronization?

options.label: AzCopy for one-time file copying
Azure File Sync for continuous synchronization with caching
Azure Data Box for physical data transfer

questions.correctLabel: Azure File Sync for continuous synchronization with caching
questions.extraHtml:
Azure File Sync continuously synchronizes files between on-premises and Azure Files with cloud tiering for on-premises caching. AzCopy is for one-time copying. Data Box is for initial bulk transfer without ongoing sync.

Reference: Azure File Sync

questions.promptHtml: Your security policy requires storage account access restricted exclusively to specific Azure virtual networks, blocking access from public internet. Which security feature implements this network-based access control?

options.label: Storage account firewall with VNet service endpoints
Shared access signatures (SAS) with time-based expiration
Storage account keys with rotation policy

questions.correctLabel: Storage account firewall with VNet service endpoints
questions.extraHtml:
Storage account firewall with VNet service endpoints restricts access to specific VNets, blocking public internet access. SAS tokens control delegation but don't restrict network access. Account keys provide authentication but not network filtering.

Reference: Storage network security

questions.promptHtml: Users should access protected web applications only from corporate-managed devices enrolled in device management. Which Conditional Access configuration enforces this device-based restriction?

options.label: Require multifactor authentication for all users
Require device to be marked as compliant
Block access from unknown locations

questions.correctLabel: Require device to be marked as compliant
questions.extraHtml:
Conditional Access device compliance check requires devices enrolled and compliant with organizational policies. MFA enhances security but doesn't verify device management. Location blocking doesn't verify device compliance.

Reference: Device-based Conditional Access

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires audit logs showing all Azure subscription changes including resource creations, deletions, and configuration modifications. Which Azure feature provides this change tracking?

options.label: Azure Monitor metrics for performance data
Azure Activity Log for subscription-level changes
Application Insights for application telemetry

questions.correctLabel: Azure Activity Log for subscription-level changes
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Activity Log records all control plane operations (create, update, delete) at subscription level. Monitor metrics track performance. Application Insights tracks application telemetry, not subscription changes.

Reference: Azure Activity Log

questions.promptHtml: Development, staging, and production environments each require separate billing, access controls, and resource limits. Which Azure organizational structure provides this isolation?

options.label: Resource groups per environment within single subscription
Separate subscriptions per environment
Tags identifying environments on all resources

questions.correctLabel: Separate subscriptions per environment
questions.extraHtml:
Subscriptions provide billing isolation, separate access controls, and independent resource quotas per environment. Resource groups within subscriptions share billing and limits. Tags provide metadata without isolation.

Reference: Subscription organization patterns

questions.promptHtml: Your application spans Azure VMs, Azure SQL Database, App Services, and Storage accounts requiring coordinated updates. Which Azure deployment approach enables versioned, atomic infrastructure updates?

options.label: Manual Azure Portal deployments
Azure Resource Manager templates with versioned updates
Azure CLI scripts executed sequentially

questions.correctLabel: Azure Resource Manager templates with versioned updates
questions.extraHtml:
ARM templates define infrastructure declaratively enabling atomic, versioned deployments with rollback capability. Manual deployments lack version control. CLI scripts are imperative and harder to version and rollback.

Reference: ARM template benefits

questions.promptHtml: A regulatory audit requires proving that only authorized personnel accessed production databases during a specific time period. Which Azure feature provides this access audit trail?

options.label: Azure RBAC role assignments showing current permissions
Azure Activity Log with sign-in and access logs
Network security group flow logs

questions.correctLabel: Azure Activity Log with sign-in and access logs
questions.extraHtml:
Activity Log combined with Entra ID sign-in logs provides comprehensive access audit trails. RBAC shows current assignments, not access history. NSG flow logs track network traffic, not identity-based access.

Reference: Azure access audit logs

questions.promptHtml: Your organization deploys resources across 20 Azure regions. Tracking costs per region for optimization requires categorizing resources. Which Azure capability enables this cross-region cost tracking?

options.label: Resource groups organizing resources per region
Tags with region metadata for cost analysis
Management groups per region

questions.correctLabel: Tags with region metadata for cost analysis
questions.extraHtml:
Tags enable flexible cost categorization across any resource dimension including region, department, or project. Resource groups organize resources but tags provide cross-cutting cost analysis. Management groups organize subscriptions, not individual resources.

Reference: Cost analysis with tags

questions.promptHtml: Your web application requires automatic deployment when code commits to the main branch, including infrastructure provisioning and application deployment. Which Azure capability enables this automated deployment pipeline?

options.label: Manual deployments via Azure Portal
Azure DevOps Pipelines with Infrastructure as Code
Azure Cloud Shell scripts executed manually

questions.correctLabel: Azure DevOps Pipelines with Infrastructure as Code
questions.extraHtml:
Azure DevOps Pipelines automate CI/CD including infrastructure provisioning (via ARM/Bicep) and application deployment triggered by source control. Manual deployments lack automation. Manual shell scripts don't provide CI/CD automation.

Reference: Azure Pipelines

questions.promptHtml: Production workloads run on VMs with 8 vCPUs but average only 20% CPU utilization. Cost optimization requires matching VM size to actual usage. Which Azure tool provides rightsizing recommendations?

options.label: Azure Monitor showing current CPU metrics
Azure Advisor with cost optimization recommendations
Azure Cost Management showing spending trends

questions.correctLabel: Azure Advisor with cost optimization recommendations
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Advisor analyzes resource utilization and provides actionable rightsizing recommendations including VM size optimization. Monitor shows metrics but doesn't provide recommendations. Cost Management shows spending but Advisor provides optimization guidance.

Reference: Advisor cost optimization

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires preventing production VMs from being stopped or deleted while allowing configuration changes like scaling or networking updates. Which lock type enables this protection?

options.label: ReadOnly lock preventing all modifications
CanNotDelete lock preventing deletion while allowing updates
Azure Policy denying delete operations

questions.correctLabel: CanNotDelete lock preventing deletion while allowing updates
questions.extraHtml:
CanNotDelete lock prevents deletion and stop operations while permitting configuration updates. ReadOnly prevents all changes including updates. Policy can audit/deny but locks provide explicit protection.

Reference: Resource lock types

questions.promptHtml: Your application uses third-party APIs with rate limits. You need observability into API call success rates, latency, and failures to optimize retry logic. Which Azure monitoring capability provides this dependency tracking?

options.label: Azure Service Health for platform status
Application Insights dependency tracking
Azure Advisor for optimization recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Application Insights dependency tracking
questions.extraHtml:
Application Insights automatically tracks dependencies including APIs, databases, and external services with success rates, latency, and failure analysis. Service Health reports Azure platform status. Advisor provides recommendations, not real-time dependency tracking.

Reference: Application Insights dependency tracking

questions.promptHtml: Users report application slowdowns during peak hours. You need to correlate high CPU usage with increased request rates to identify bottlenecks. Which Azure service aggregates these performance metrics for analysis?

options.label: Azure Activity Log for operational changes
Azure Monitor with metrics and correlation
Azure Service Health for incident notifications

questions.correctLabel: Azure Monitor with metrics and correlation
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Monitor collects metrics from multiple sources enabling correlation analysis between CPU, requests, and other telemetry. Activity Log tracks operations, not performance metrics. Service Health reports platform issues.

Reference: Azure Monitor metrics

questions.promptHtml: A critical production database must never be deleted by any user including administrators. Configuration changes are allowed. Which Azure protection mechanism enforces this strictest deletion prevention?

options.label: Azure RBAC removing all delete permissions
Resource lock set to CanNotDelete on the database
Azure Policy auditing delete attempts

questions.correctLabel: Resource lock set to CanNotDelete on the database
questions.extraHtml:
Resource locks provide the strictest deletion protection overriding even owner permissions. RBAC can be modified by owners. Azure Policy can audit but locks provide enforcement.

Reference: Resource lock enforcement

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires all Azure SQL Databases use Transparent Data Encryption and require MFA for admin access. Automated auditing should flag non-compliant resources. Which Azure service provides this policy-based compliance auditing?

options.label: Azure Monitor for metric collection
Azure Policy with compliance evaluation
Azure Security Center classic features

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with compliance evaluation
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Policy evaluates resources against policy definitions, providing compliance dashboards and audit reports. Monitor collects metrics but doesn't evaluate compliance. Security Center is now Defender for Cloud focused on threat protection.

Reference: Azure Policy compliance

questions.promptHtml: Your company acquires another organization with their own Azure tenant. You need unified management of subscriptions across both tenants under centralized policies. Which Azure feature enables cross-tenant subscription management?

options.label: Azure Lighthouse for delegated resource management
Virtual network peering across tenants
Azure AD B2B guest access

questions.correctLabel: Azure Lighthouse for delegated resource management
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Lighthouse enables cross-tenant resource management with centralized policies and RBAC. VNet peering connects networks but doesn't provide subscription management. B2B guest access grants user access but not subscription-level management.

Reference: Azure Lighthouse

questions.promptHtml: Your web application is deployed across 3 availability zones. You need a single HTTP endpoint that distributes traffic across zones with automatic health checks. Which Azure service provides this zone-redundant load balancing?

options.label: Azure Traffic Manager for global DNS routing
Azure Load Balancer with zone-redundant frontend
Azure Front Door for global load balancing

questions.correctLabel: Azure Load Balancer with zone-redundant frontend
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Load Balancer with zone-redundant frontend IP distributes traffic across availability zones within a region with automatic health probes. Traffic Manager provides global DNS routing. Front Door is for global edge load balancing.

Reference: Zone-redundant Load Balancer

questions.promptHtml: Your organization requires that all virtual machines must have antimalware protection enabled and monitoring agents installed before deployment. Which Azure Policy effect enforces this preventive requirement?

options.label: Audit effect to report non-compliance after deployment
DeployIfNotExists effect to automatically add required extensions
Deny effect to block VM creation entirely

questions.correctLabel: DeployIfNotExists effect to automatically add required extensions
questions.extraHtml:
DeployIfNotExists policy effect automatically deploys required configurations (like agents) if missing, ensuring compliance. Audit only reports non-compliance. Deny blocks creation but doesn't auto-remediate.

Reference: Azure Policy DeployIfNotExists

questions.promptHtml: A disaster recovery plan requires databases failover to a secondary region if the primary region becomes unavailable. The secondary region must be within 300 miles of the primary. Which Azure feature provides this paired region disaster recovery?

options.label: Availability zones within single region
Azure region pairs with predefined failover relationships
Custom region selection for geo-replication

questions.correctLabel: Azure region pairs with predefined failover relationships
questions.extraHtml:
Azure region pairs are geographically separated with prioritized recovery orchestration and sequential updates. Availability zones provide datacenter-level redundancy within a region. Custom selection doesn't provide the platform-managed benefits of paired regions.

Reference: Azure region pair benefits

questions.promptHtml: Your application requires passwordless authentication using biometrics or hardware security keys, eliminating password-based attacks. Which Azure authentication method supports this passwordless approach?

options.label: Username and password with complexity requirements
Microsoft Authenticator app with passwordless phone sign-in
Security questions for account recovery

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Authenticator app with passwordless phone sign-in
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft Authenticator supports passwordless authentication using biometrics or PIN, eliminating password vulnerabilities. Traditional passwords remain vulnerable. Security questions provide recovery but aren't primary passwordless authentication.

Reference: Azure passwordless authentication

questions.promptHtml: Your global application serves users in 30 countries. Each user should connect to the nearest Azure region for optimal performance. Which Azure service provides geographic traffic routing to the closest endpoint?

options.label: Azure Load Balancer for regional load balancing
Azure Traffic Manager with performance routing
Azure Application Gateway with URL routing

questions.correctLabel: Azure Traffic Manager with performance routing
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Traffic Manager provides DNS-based global traffic routing with performance-based policies directing users to nearest endpoints. Load Balancer operates within a region. Application Gateway provides Layer 7 routing within a region.

Reference: Traffic Manager performance routing

questions.promptHtml: A compliance audit requires demonstrating that sensitivity labels are applied to all financial documents in Azure, with classification tracking across storage accounts and databases. Which Azure service provides this data classification governance?

options.label: Azure Policy for resource compliance
Microsoft Purview for data classification and cataloging
Azure Tags for resource metadata

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Purview for data classification and cataloging
questions.extraHtml:
Microsoft Purview provides data classification with sensitivity labels, data lineage, and cataloging across Azure data sources. Azure Policy governs resource configurations. Tags provide resource metadata but not data classification.

Reference: Purview data classification

questions.promptHtml: Your infrastructure as code templates must be stored in version control with approval workflows before deployment. Which Azure service provides git-based source control for ARM templates?

options.label: Azure Repos with git repositories
Azure Cloud Shell for template storage
Azure Portal template library

questions.correctLabel: Azure Repos with git repositories
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Repos provides git-based source control with pull requests, approvals, and version history for templates. Cloud Shell is for execution, not source control. Portal template library doesn't provide version control workflows.

Reference: Azure Repos

questions.promptHtml: Your organization must ensure all resources deployed to Azure comply with ISO 27001 security controls. Which Azure capability validates resource configurations against compliance standards?

options.label: Azure Policy with regulatory compliance built-in initiatives
Azure Monitor for security metrics
Azure Advisor for security recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with regulatory compliance built-in initiatives
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Policy includes built-in compliance initiatives for regulatory standards (ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA) with automatic compliance evaluation. Monitor collects metrics. Advisor provides recommendations but not compliance evaluation.

Reference: Azure Policy regulatory compliance

questions.promptHtml: A database must replicate data synchronously across 3 datacenters within a region to survive any single datacenter failure with zero data loss. Which Azure storage redundancy provides this in-region synchronous replication?

options.label: Locally redundant storage (LRS) within single datacenter
Zone-redundant storage (ZRS) across availability zones
Geo-redundant storage (GRS) with asynchronous replication

questions.correctLabel: Zone-redundant storage (ZRS) across availability zones
questions.extraHtml:
ZRS replicates synchronously across three availability zones (datacenters) within a region, ensuring zero data loss on datacenter failure. LRS replicates within one datacenter. GRS replicates across regions asynchronously.

Reference: ZRS synchronous replication

questions.promptHtml: Your application experiences a critical outage. You need immediate notification of all Azure service incidents affecting your resources. Which Azure service provides this personalized service health alerting?

options.label: Azure Monitor alerts for resource metrics
Azure Service Health with custom health alerts
Azure Advisor for service recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Service Health with custom health alerts
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Service Health provides personalized service health alerts for incidents, planned maintenance, and health advisories affecting your subscriptions. Monitor tracks resource metrics. Advisor provides optimization recommendations.

Reference: Service Health alerts

questions.promptHtml: Your organization uses Azure exclusively but manages Kubernetes clusters, databases, and VMs across AWS and Google Cloud. You need unified Azure management capabilities across all cloud providers. Which Azure service extends Azure control plane to multi-cloud resources?

options.label: Azure Monitor for multi-cloud observability
Azure Arc for multi-cloud management
Azure Policy for resource governance

questions.correctLabel: Azure Arc for multi-cloud management
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Arc extends Azure Resource Manager, Policy, and management capabilities to resources outside Azure including AWS, GCP, and on-premises. Monitor provides observability. Policy governs Azure resources but Arc enables policy across clouds.

Reference: Azure Arc multi-cloud

questions.promptHtml: A web application must authenticate users and immediately block sign-in attempts from anonymous proxy servers or Tor networks. Which Conditional Access condition detects and blocks these risky sign-in locations?

options.label: Named locations defining trusted IP ranges
Sign-in risk detection with high-risk blocking
Device compliance requiring managed devices

questions.correctLabel: Sign-in risk detection with high-risk blocking
questions.extraHtml:
Sign-in risk detection identifies anomalous sign-in patterns including anonymous proxies and Tor, enabling automatic blocking. Named locations define trusted IPs but don't detect proxies. Device compliance verifies device state, not sign-in risk.

Reference: Entra sign-in risk detection
